Item 1. Business
The Boeing Company, together with its subsidiaries (herein referred to as "Boeing," the "Company," "we," "us," "our"), is one of the world's major aerospace firms.
We are organized based on the products and services we offer. We operate in four reportable segments:
- 
Commercial Airplanes (BCA);
- 
Defense, Space & Security (BDS);
- 
Global Services (BGS);
- 
Boeing Capital (BCC).
Commercial Airplanes Segment
This segment develops, produces and markets commercial jet aircraft principally to the commercial airline industry worldwide. We are a leading producer of commercial aircraft and offer a family of commercial jetliners designed to meet a broad spectrum of global passenger and cargo requirements of airlines. This family of commercial jet aircraft in production includes the 737 narrow-body model and the 767, 777 and 787 wide-body models. We ended production of the 747 wide-body model in 2022. Development continues on the 777X program and the 737-7 and 737-10 derivatives.
Defense, Space & Security Segment
This segment engages in the research, development, production and modification of manned and unmanned military aircraft and weapons systems for strike, surveillance and mobility, including fighter and trainer aircraft; vertical lift, including rotorcraft and tilt-ro
…[truncated 9031 chars]…
ion (FAA) and the Department of Homeland Security. Similar government authorities exist in our non-U.S. markets.
Government Contracts.
The U.S. government, and other governments, may terminate any of our government contracts at their convenience, as well as for default based on our failure to meet specified performance requirements. If any of our U.S. government contracts were to be terminated for convenience, we generally would be entitled to receive payment for work completed and allowable termination or cancellation costs. If any of our government contracts were to be terminated for default, generally the U.S. government would pay only for the work that has been accepted and could require us to pay the difference between the original contract price and the cost to re-procure the contract items, net of the work accepted from the original contract. The U.S. government can also hold us liable for damages resulting from the default.
Commercial Aircraft.
In the U.S., our commercial aircraft products are required to comply with FAA regulations governing production and quality systems, airworthiness and installation approvals, repair procedures and continuing operational safety. New aircraft models and new derivative aircraft are required to obtain FAA certification prior to entry into service. Outside the U.S., similar requirements exist for airworthiness, installation and operational approvals. These requirements are generally administered by the national aviation authorities of each country and, in the case of Europe, coordinated by the European Union Aviation Safety Agency.
Environmental.
We are subject to various federal, state, local and non-U.S. laws and regulations relating to environmental protection, including the discharge, treatment, storage, disposal and remediation of hazardous substances and wastes. We could also be affected by laws and regulations relating to climate change, including laws limiting or otherwise related to greenhouse gas emissions. These laws and regulations could lead to increased environmental compliance expenditures, increased energy and raw materials costs and new and/or additional investment in designs and technologies. We continually assess our compliance status and management of environmental matters to ensure our operations are in compliance with all applicable environmental laws and regulations. Investigation, remediation and operation and maintenance costs associated with environmental compliance and management of sites are a normal, recurring part of our operations. These costs often are allowable costs under our contracts with the U.S. government. It is reasonably possible that costs incurred to ensure continued environmental compliance could have a material impact on our results of operations, financial condition or cash flows if additional work requirements or more stringent clean-up standards are imposed by regulators, new areas of soil, air and groundwater contamination are discovered and/or expansions of work scope are prompted by the results of investigations.
For additional information relating to environmental contingencies, see Note 13 to our Consolidated Financial Statements.
Non-U.S. Sales.
Our non-U.S. sales are subject to both U.S. and non-U.S. governmental regulations and procurement policies and practices, including regulations relating to import-export control, tariffs, investment, exchange controls, anti-corruption and repatriation of earnings. Non-U.S. sales are also subject to varying currency, political and economic risks.
Raw Materials, Parts and Subassemblies
We are highly dependent on the availability of essential materials, parts and subassemblies from our suppliers and subcontractors. The most important raw materials required for our aerospace products
4
Table of Contents
are aluminum (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions), titanium (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions) and composites (including carbon and boron). Although alternative sources generally exist for these raw materials, qualification of the sources could take a year or more. During 2022, as a result of the Russia Ukraine war, we suspended purchasing titanium from Russia. This has not disrupted our operations as we have been able to use inventory on hand and identify alternative sources. Many major components and product equipment items are procured or subcontracted on a sole-source basis. We continue to work with a small number of sole-source suppliers to ensure continuity of supply for certain items.
Suppliers
We are dependent upon the ability of a large number of U.S. and non-U.S. suppliers and subcontractors to meet performance specifications, quality standards and delivery schedules at our anticipated costs. While we maintain an extensive qualification and performance surveillance system to control risk associated with such reliance on third parties, failure of suppliers or subcontractors to meet commitments could adversely affect production schedules and program/contract profitability, thereby jeopardizing our ability to fulfill commitments to our customers. We are also dependent on the availability of energy sources, such as electricity, at affordable prices.
Seasonality
No material portion of our business is considered to be seasonal.
Executive Officers of the Registrant
See "Item 10. Directors, Executive Officers and Corporate Governance" in Part III.
Other Information
Boeing was originally incorporated in the State of Washington in 1916 and reincorporated in Delaware in 1934. Our principal executive offices are located at 929 Long Bridge Drive, Arlington, Virginia 22202, and our telephone number is (703) 465-3500.
General information about us can be found at www.boeing.com. The information contained on or connected to our website is not incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K and should not be considered part of this or any other report filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Our Annual Report on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q and Current Reports on Form 8-K, as well as any amendments to those reports, are available free of charge through our website as soon as reasonably practicable after we file them with, or furnish them to, the SEC. The SEC maintains a website at www.sec.gov that contains reports, proxy statements and other information regarding SEC registrants, including Boeing.
Forward-Looking Statements
This report, as well as our annual report to shareholders, quarterly reports and other filings we make with the SEC, press and earnings releases and other written and oral communications, contain "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. Words such as "may," "should," "expects," "intends," "projects," "plans," "believes," "estimates," "targets," "anticipates" and similar expressions generally identify these forward-looking statements. Examples of forward-looking statements include statements relating to our future financial condition and operating results, as well as any other statement that does not directly relate to any historical or current fact.

Many factors, including those set forth in the "Risk Factors" section below and other important factors disclosed in this report and from time to time in our other filings with the SEC, could cause actual results to differ materially and adversely from these forward-looking statements. Any forward-looking statement speaks only as of the date on which it is made, and we assume no obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statement whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise, except as required by law.
BA.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: Boeing (BA) as of 2024-04-01**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
- **Revenue:** Latest Q4 2023 revenue was $22.02B. TTM revenue (sum of last 4 quarters) is $77.79B. Quarterly trajectory shows volatility: Q1 2022 ($13.99B) to Q4 2023 ($22.02B). YoY Q4 revenue growth was +10.2%.
- **Earnings:** Persistent losses. Latest Q4 2023 EPS diluted was -$0.03. TTM net income is -$2.24B. The only positive EPS in the sequence was Q2 2022 ($0.32).
- **Margins:** Latest Q4 2023 gross margin was 12.3% ($2.70B gross profit / $22.02B revenue). Operating margin was 1.3% ($283.00M operating income). TTM operating income is -$773.00M.
- **Cash-Flow:** Unavailable for all supplied quarters.
- **Balance Sheet:** Negative equity persists, latest at -$17.23B (Q4 2023). Total debt decreased from $57.56B (Q1 2022) to $52.05B (Q4 2023). Total assets were $137.01B (Q4 2023).

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality:** Low. Q4 2023 operating income of $283.00M resulted in a net loss of -$30.00M, indicating non-operating costs. The TTM operating income is negative (-$773.00M).
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Very weak. Total equity has been negative for all eight supplied quarters, indicating a deficit. Latest Q4 2023 shows total liabilities of $154.24B exceeding total assets of $137.01B.

**3. Valuation vs. History**
- Primary available valuation metric is P/S at 1.503. Traditional earnings-based ratios (P/E, EV/EBIT, FCF yield) are not applicable due to negative/missing earnings and cash flow.
- Net margin trajectory improved from -7.6% (2023-04-03) to -2.9% (2024-03-28), but remains negative.

**4. Price Level & Return Context**
- **Latest Price:** $192.99 (2024-03-28).
- **Returns:** Price declined -26.4% over the last quarter but is down only -5.8% over the last year.
- **Price vs. Fundamentals:** The recent quarterly price decline of -26.4% occurred alongside a QoQ revenue increase of +21.6% but an EPS decline of -98.9%. The annual price decline of -5.8% is modest versus a TTM net loss of -$2.24B.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
- **Upside:** Revenue growth showed a strong QoQ rebound of +21.6% in Q4 2023. If operating margins turn sustainably positive, earnings recovery could be significant given the $22B quarterly revenue base.
- **Downside:** Persistent negative equity (-$17.23B) and debt load ($52.05B) create balance-sheet risk. Continued net losses (e.g., TTM of -$2.24B) could worsen the equity deficit further.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The stock trades at a P/S of 1.50 on TTM revenue of $77.79B. The fundamental path is characterized by volatile revenue, persistent losses, and a severely impaired balance sheet with negative equity. The recent quarterly price drop of -26.4% partially reflects poor earnings but may not fully price the balance-sheet risk, given the lack of positive earnings-based valuation anchors. The quantitative setup presents high risk: continued losses could intensify balance-sheet stress, while any sustained return to profitability would represent a major inflection. The risk of fundamental deterioration currently outweighs the valuation based on sales alone.
BA.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: Boeing (BA)**
**Decision Date: 2024-04-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read (from 2023-01-27 10-K)**
Boeing is a major global aerospace company organized into four segments: Commercial Airplanes (BCA), Defense, Space & Security (BDS), Global Services (BGS), and Boeing Capital (BCC). BCA is a leading producer of commercial jetliners (737, 767, 777, 787 families) and is developing the 777X and 737 derivatives. BDS provides military aircraft, weapons systems, and space products. BGS offers a full spectrum of lifecycle services. The company operates in a highly competitive environment, facing rivals like Airbus in commercial aviation and Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, and others in defense. Its operations are heavily regulated, particularly by the FAA for commercial aircraft, and it is dependent on a large, global supply chain.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated, from Supplied Text)**
*   **Management Overhaul (2024-03-26):** CEO David changes were announced, including the planned departure of CEO David Calhoun and Chairman Larry Kellner, following a federal investigation into a January 2024 incident where a door panel blew out of a 737 MAX 9 in mid-flight.
*   **Contract Win (2024-03-27):** Boeing secured a $96.2 million contract for the MH-47G helicopter, indicating continued activity in the military rotorcraft market.
*   **Analyst Sentiment (2024-03-27, 2024-03-28):** Wall Street analysts expressed views on the management changes and delivery concerns. One analyst (Citi) set a price target implying 31% upside, citing confidence in a recovery despite quality control issues.
*   **Regulatory & Legal Scrutiny (2024-03-20, 2024-03-21):** The FAA is conducting a comprehensive audit of the 737 MAX 9 production line and suppliers. Shareholder lawsuits have been filed, and a former quality-control inspector alleged excessive defects at a supplier plant.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Operational & Quality Control:** The January 2024 door plug incident has intensified scrutiny on Boeing's manufacturing and quality processes, leading to FAA audits and allegations of defects (2024-03-20, 2024-03-21).
    *   **Legal & Regulatory:** The company faces ongoing federal investigations, shareholder lawsuits, and potential for further regulatory action, which could result in financial penalties, increased oversight, and reputational damage (2024-02-22, 2024-03-04, 2024-03-06).
    *   **Execution & Delivery:** Management changes and the focus on quality may disrupt near-term production and delivery schedules, potentially affecting financial performance and customer relationships.
*   **Offsets:**
    *   **Strong Backlog:** The company reported a record backlog of $520 billion as of its 2023 Q4 report (2024-02-13), providing long-term revenue visibility.
    *   **Management Reset:** The leadership overhaul is viewed by some analysts as a potential crucial turning point to address cultural and operational issues (2024-03-26).
    *   **Diversified Portfolio:** The business spans commercial, defense, and services, which may provide some stability. Recent contract wins in defense (2024-03-27) demonstrate ongoing demand in that segment.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral to Slightly Negative.** The franchise remains a dominant aerospace player with a massive backlog, providing a long-term foundation. However, the near-term narrative is dominated by severe operational and quality control crises stemming from the January 2024 incident. This has triggered intense regulatory scrutiny, legal actions, and a necessary but disruptive management overhaul. While the leadership change is a potential positive catalyst, the path to restoring trust and ensuring consistent production quality is uncertain and fraught with risk. The confirmed evidence points to a company in a period of significant stress and remediation.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Boeing's massive $520B backlog and dominant aerospace franchise provide long-term support, but persistent negative equity (-$17.23B), TTM net losses of -$2.24B, and severe operational/quality crises following the January 2024 door plug incident create substantial near-term risk. The stock's recent -26.4% quarterly decline partially reflects these headwinds, but without positive earnings anchors, the risk-reward is roughly balanced at current levels.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Boeing's massive $520B backlog and dominant aerospace franchise provide long-term support, but persistent negative equity (-$17.23B), TTM net losses of -$2.24B, and severe operational/quality crises following the January 2024 door plug incident create substantial near-term risk. The stock's recent -26.4% quarterly decline partially reflects these headwinds, but without positive earnings anchors, the risk-reward is roughly balanced at current levels.

Record backlog of $520B provides long-term revenue visibility, but near-term production and delivery face disruption from FAA audits and quality control remediation following the January 2024 737 MAX 9 door plug incident
Management overhaul announced 2024-03-26 with CEO and Chairman departures represents a potential turning point but introduces execution uncertainty during a critical operational recovery period
P/S of 1.50 on TTM revenue of $77.79B is the only viable valuation metric given persistent losses, offering limited margin of safety without a clear earnings inflection point

ITEM 1. BUSINESS
GENERAL
NIKE, Inc. was incorporated in 1967 under the laws of the State of Oregon. As used in this Annual Report on Form 10-K (this "Annual Report"), the terms "we," "us," "our," "NIKE" and the "Company" refer to NIKE, Inc. and its predecessors, subsidiaries and affiliates, collectively, unless the context indicates otherwise.
Our principal business activity is the design, development and worldwide marketing and selling of athletic footwear, apparel, equipment, accessories and services. NIKE is the largest seller of athletic footwear and apparel in the world. We sell our products through NIKE Direct operations, which are comprised of both NIKE-owned retail stores and sales through our digital platforms (also referred to as "NIKE Brand Digital"), to retail accounts and to a mix of independent distributors, licensees and sales representatives in nearly all countries around the world. We also offer interactive consumer services and experiences through our digital platforms. Nearly all of our products are manufactured by independent contractors. Nearly all footwear and apparel products are manufactured outside the United States, while equipment products are manufactured both in the United States and abroad.
All references to fiscal 2023, 2022, 2021 and 2020 are to NIKE, Inc.'s fiscal years ended May 31, 2023, 2022, 2021 and 2020, respectively. Any references to other fiscal years refer to a fiscal year ending on May 31 of that year.
PRODUCTS
Our NIKE Brand product offerings are aligned around our consumer construct focused on Men's, Women's and Kids'. We also design products specifically for the Jordan Brand and Converse. We believe this approach allows us to create products that better meet individual consumer needs while accelerating our largest growth opportunities.
NIKE's athletic footwear products are designed primarily for specific athletic use, although a large percentage of the products are worn for casual or leisure purposes. We place considerable emphasis on innovation and high-quality construction in the development and manufacturing of our products. Our Men's, Women's and Jordan Brand footwear products currently lead in footwear sales and we expect them to continue to do so.
We also sell sports apparel, which features the same trademarks and are sold predominantly through the same marketing and distribution channels as athletic footwear. Our sports apparel, similar to our athletic footwear products, is designed primarily for athleti
…[truncated 19516 chars]…
nd obtained trademark registrations.
We have copyright protection in our designs, graphics, software applications, digital goods and other original works. When appropriate, we also obtain registered copyrights.
We file for, own and maintain many U.S. and foreign utility and design patents protecting components, technologies, materials, manufacturing techniques, features, functionality, and industrial designs used in and for the manufacture of various athletic, performance, and leisure footwear and apparel, including physical and digital versions thereof, athletic equipment, and digital devices, and related software applications. These patents expire at various times.
We believe our success depends upon our capabilities in areas such as design, research and development, production and marketing and is supported and protected by our intellectual property rights, such as trademarks, utility and design patents, copyrights, and trade secrets, among others.
We have followed a policy of applying for and registering intellectual property rights in the United States and select foreign countries on trademarks, inventions, innovations and designs that we deem valuable. We also continue to vigorously protect our intellectual property, including trademarks, patents and trade secrets against third-party infringement and misappropriation.
2023 FORM 10-K   5
Table of Contents
HUMAN CAPITAL RESOURCES
At NIKE, we consider the strength and effective management of our workforce to be essential to the ongoing success of our business. We believe that it is important to attract, develop and retain a diverse and engaged workforce at all levels of our business and that such a workforce fosters creativity and accelerates innovation. We are focused on building an increasingly diverse talent pipeline that reflects our consumers, athletes and the communities we serve.
CULTURE
Each employee shapes NIKE's culture through behaviors and practices. This starts with our Maxims, which represent our core values and, along with our Code of Conduct, feature the fundamental behaviors that help anchor, inform and guide us and apply to all employees. Our mission is to bring inspiration and innovation to every athlete in the world, which includes the belief that if you have a body, you are an athlete. We aim to do this by creating groundbreaking sport innovations, making our products more sustainably, building a creative and diverse global team, supporting the well-being of our employees and making a positive impact in communities where we live and work. Our mission is aligned with our deep commitment to maintaining an environment where all NIKE employees have the opportunity to reach their full potential, to connect to our brands and to shape our workplace culture. We believe providing for growth and retention of our employees is essential in fostering such a culture and are dedicated to giving access to training programs and career development opportunities, including trainings on NIKE's values, history and business, trainings on developing leadership skills at all levels, tools and resources for managers and qualified tuition reimbursement opportunities.
As part of our commitment to empowering our employees to help shape our culture, we source employee feedback through our Engagement Survey program, including several corporate pulse surveys. The program provides every employee throughout the globe an opportunity to provide confidential feedback on key areas known to drive employee engagement, including their satisfaction with their managers, their work and the Company generally. The program also measures our employees' emotional commitment to NIKE as well as NIKE's culture of diversity, equity and inclusion. NIKE also provides multiple points of contact for employees to speak up if they experience something that does not align with our values or otherwise violates our workplace policies, even if they are uncertain what they observed or heard is a violation of company policy.
As part of our commitment to make a positive impact on our communities, we maintain a goal of investing 2% of our prior fiscal year's pre-tax income into global communities. The focus of this investment continues to be inspiring kids to be active through play and sport as well as uniting and inspiring communities to create a better and more equitable future for all. Our community investments are an important part of our culture in that we also support employees in giving back to community organizations through donations and volunteering, which are matched by the NIKE Foundation where eligible.
EMPLOYEE BASE
As of May 31, 2023, we had approximately 83,700 employees worldwide, including retail and part-time employees. We also utilize independent contractors and temporary personnel to supplement our workforce.
None of our employees are represented by a union, except certain employees in the EMEA and APLA geographies are members of and/or represented by trade unions, as allowed or required by local law and/or collective bargaining agreements. Also, in some countries outside of the United States, local laws require employee representation by works councils (which may be entitled to information and consultation on certain subsidiary decisions) or by organizations similar to a union. In certain European countries, we are required by local law to enter into, and/or comply with, industry-wide or national collective bargaining agreements. NIKE has never experienced a material interruption of operations due to labor disagreements.
DIVERSITY, EQUITY AND INCLUSION
Diversity, equity and inclusion ("DE&I") is a strategic priority for NIKE and we are committed to having an increasingly diverse team and culture. We aim to foster an inclusive and accessible workplace through recruitment, development and retention of diverse talent with the goal of expanding representation across all dimensions of diversity over the long term. We remain committed to the targets announced in fiscal 2021 for the Company to work toward by fiscal 2025, including increasing representation of women in our global corporate workforce and leadership positions, as well as increasing representation of U.S. racial and ethnic minorities in our U.S. corporate workforce and at the Director level and above.
We continue to enhance our efforts to recruit diverse talent through our traditional channels and through initiatives, such as partnerships with athletes and sports-related organizations to create apprenticeship programs and new partnerships with organizations, colleges and universities that serve diverse populations. Additionally, we are prioritizing DE&I education so that all NIKE employees and leaders have the cultural awareness and understanding to lead inclusively and build diverse and inclusive teams. We also have Employee Networks, collectively known as NikeUNITED, representing various employee groups.
2023 FORM 10-K       6
Table of Contents
Our DE&I focus extends beyond our workforce and includes our communities, which we support in a number of ways. We have committed to investments that aim to address racial inequality and improve diversity and representation in our communities. We also are leveraging our global scale to accelerate business diversity, including investing in business training programs for women and increasing the proportion of services supplied by minority-owned businesses.
COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS
NIKE's total rewards are intended to be competitive and equitable, meet the diverse needs of our global teammates and reinforce our values. We are committed to providing comprehensive, competitive and equitable pay and benefits to our employees, and we have invested, and aim to continue to invest, in our employees through growth and development and holistic well-being initiatives. Our initiatives in this area include:

**Quantitative Equity Report: NKE**
**Decision Date: 2024-04-01 | Data Cutoff: 2024-03-28**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
*   **Revenue & Earnings:** TTM revenue (ending 2023-11-30) is $51.54B. Latest quarterly revenue (Q2 2023) is $13.39B, a QoQ increase of +3.5% and YoY increase of +0.5%. TTM net income is $5.30B. Latest quarterly EPS is $1.03, a QoQ increase of +9.6% and YoY increase of +21.2%.
*   **Margins:** Gross margin is 44.0%. Operating margin is 12.2%. Net margin is 10.3%.
*   **Cash Flow:** Free cash flow data is unavailable for all reported periods.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total equity is $14.15B. Total assets are $37.20B. Note: A data inconsistency exists in recent periods where total liabilities are reported as negative, conflicting with the accounting identity (Assets = Liabilities + Equity). Total debt as of 2023-05-31 was $8.93B.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** Margins have been relatively stable (net margin range 0.098-0.115 over the past year). The YoY EPS growth of +21.2% on +0.5% revenue growth indicates margin expansion. However, the absence of cash flow data prevents assessment of cash conversion quality.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** The provided liability figures contain anomalies (e.g., negative values in recent quarters), making leverage ratios unreliable. The available debt figure ($8.93B) is moderate relative to equity ($14.00B at the same date).

**3. Valuation vs. Company History**
*   **Ratios:** Latest PE (diluted) is 27.17. P/B is 10.18. P/S is 2.79. Earnings yield is 3.7%.
*   **Historical Context:** Over the past year, the PE ratio ranged from 26.8 to 37.5. The current PE of 27.17 is at the very low end of this 365-day window. P/B also sits at its 1-year low of 10.18 (range: 10.0 - 13.8).

**4. Price Level & Returns vs. Fundamentals**
*   **Price:** Close on 2024-03-28 was $93.98. The 52-week range is $88.66 to $128.68.
*   **Returns:** The stock returned -20.3% over the past year and -12.3% over the last quarter. This contrasts with the positive TTM earnings trajectory (TTM net income: $5.30B).
*   **Path:** The quarterly path shows significant volatility: a +19.8% gain in Q3 2023 followed by a -12.3% loss in Q4 2023.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Upside:** Valuation is at multi-year lows on a PE basis (27.17 vs. 1-year high of 37.5). A reversion to the mean PE would imply significant capital appreciation. Recent earnings growth (YoY EPS +21.2%) demonstrates operational improvement.
*   **Downside:** The price has declined sharply (-20.3% YoY) despite earnings growth, indicating persistent market skepticism. Free cash flow generation is unquantifiable, posing a fundamental assessment risk. Revenue growth is minimal (+0.5% YoY). Balance sheet data inconsistencies create uncertainty.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The stock trades at the low end of its historical valuation range (PE: 27.17) alongside modest revenue growth but strong recent earnings expansion. The significant price decline (-20.3% YoY) has compressed multiples. The risk/reward appears moderately favorable for a valuation-based thesis, contingent on the durability of earnings improvement. The primary quantitative risk is the complete absence of cash flow data to validate earnings quality, alongside anomalous balance sheet figures that limit a full financial health assessment.
NKE.qualitative_report:
### Nike (NKE) Qualitative Assessment Report
**Decision Date:** 2024-04-01
**Evidence Dates:** 2023-07-20 (10-K) to 2024-03-31 (news)

---

#### 1. Business/Franchise Read
Nike is the world's largest seller of athletic footwear and apparel, with a global franchise built on innovation, design, and marketing. The company operates through direct-to-consumer (NIKE Direct) and wholesale channels across nearly all countries. Its brand portfolio is anchored by the NIKE and Jordan brands, with Converse as a separate reportable segment. The 10-K (2023-07-20) states the company's success depends on continuous innovation, marketing, and responding to consumer trends. Its business is highly competitive, facing rivals like adidas, Lululemon, and New Balance.

#### 2. Recent Developments (Dated Only)
*   **Positive Product Response (2024-03-27):** A Wedbush analyst noted the successful launch of the new Air Max DN model during "Air Max Day" provided "much-needed good news" after recent share weakness. The analyst maintained an Outperform rating and $115 price target.
*   **Positive Industry Forecast (2024-03-27, 2024-03-28):** Analyst commentary (from Zacks and Wedbush) highlighted anticipation for an "acceleration of innovation" and product launches to drive growth in the second half of 2024 and into 2025.
*   **Earnout of Bullish Outlook (2024-03-22):** Post-earnings call, analyst commentary emphasized management's plan to drive long-term growth through innovation and elevating the consumer experience despite near-term headwinds.
*   **Broker Price Target Adjustment (2024-03-22):** Goldman Sachs reduced its stock price target to $120 but maintained a buy rating, citing expected inventory improvement and innovation acceleration.
*   **Overall Negative Sentiment (2024-03-28 - 2024-03-31):** Multiple news articles characterized NKE as a stock "on sale" or "waving a red flag," indicating market frustration with its recent performance compared to broader market gains.
*   **Supply Chain Context (2023-07-20):** The 10-K noted that fiscal 2023 (ended May 2023) started with supply chain volatility that improved later in the year, alongside inflationary cost pressures.

#### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks (from text):**
*   **Intense Competition:** The 10-K states the industry is "highly competitive," a "significant risk factor." Recent articles (2024-03-29, 2024-03-30) directly compare NKE's growth unfavorably to competitors like Lululemon.
*   **Consumer Preference & Innovation Execution:** The 10-K identifies the risk of failing to respond to trends, which could materially adversely impact sales. Market commentary (2024-03-27) cited investor frustration over a perceived "lack of exciting new products" as a reason for share underperformance.
*   **Supply Chain & Cost Pressures:** The 10-K (2023-07-20) acknowledges supply chain volatility and inflationary pressures on costs, with ongoing "protectionist measures" potentially increasing product costs.
*   **Trademark Litigation:** Active lawsuits defending trademarks are confirmed (e.g., BAPE lawsuit proceeding as of 2024-03-06). While companies vigorously protect IP, litigation presents operational and reputational risk.

**Offsets (from text):**
*   **Strong Brand & Scale NIKE (2023-07-20):** The 10-K asserts the company is the global category leader and believes it is competitive across key factors like innovation, marketing, and distribution.
*   **Innovation Pipeline:** Multiple analyst notes (2024-03-22, 2024-03-27) and the company's own earnings call (2024-03-22) emphasize a planned acceleration of innovation and product launches slated for late 2024 and 2025.
*   **Inventory Improvement:** Analyst commentary (2024-03-22) identified a "strengthening inventory situation" within the industry as a potential positive.

#### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral to Slightly Negative.**

The **confirmed evidence** presents a mixed picture. Nike's fundamental franchise as the global category leader in athletic wear remains intact, supported by its scale, brand, and a management team actively planning an innovation-led recovery (2024-03-22). However, the **recent narrative** (March 2024) is dominated by acknowledged near-term struggles: share price underperformance, comparisons lagging competitors, and market sentiment labeling the stock as distressed or a potential trap. The successful Air Max DN launch is a positive data point but has not yet shifted the prevailing negative discourse. Key risks around intense competition and the need to flawlessly execute a product innovation cycle are strongly emphasized in both the 10-K and recent market commentary. The qualitative outlook is therefore cautious, awaiting proof that the announced innovation pipeline can reverse the recent negative momentum.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Nike trades at a historically low valuation (PE 27.17 vs. 1-year range 26.8-37.5) with recent earnings growth (+21.2% YoY EPS), but minimal revenue growth (+0.5% YoY), persistent negative market sentiment, and unverifiable cash flow quality limit conviction in a near-term reversion thesis.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Nike trades at a historically low valuation (PE 27.17 vs. 1-year range 26.8-37.5) with recent earnings growth (+21.2% YoY EPS), but minimal revenue growth (+0.5% YoY), persistent negative market sentiment, and unverifiable cash flow quality limit conviction in a near-term reversion thesis.
key_factors: Valuation at 1-year low: PE of 27.17 sits at the bottom of its 365-day range (26.8-37.5), with P/B also at 1-year low of 10.18, suggesting the market has priced in significant pessimism.

Earnings growth despite revenue stagnation: YoY EPS growth of +21.2% on only +0.5% YoY revenue growth indicates margin expansion, but this dynamic may not be sustainable without top-line acceleration.

Sharp price decline vs. positive earnings: Stock returned -20.3% over the past year and -12.3% over the last quarter despite TTM net income of $5.30B, reflecting deep market skepticism about Nike's competitive positioning versus rivals like Lululemon.

Innovation catalyst unproven: Management and analysts (2024-03-22, 2024-03-27) highlight an acceleration of product launches for late 2024/2025, including the successful Air Max DN, but the qualitative narrative remains dominated by concerns over execution and competition.

Item 1. Business
This Annual Report on Form 10-K and the documents incorporated herein by reference contain forward-looking statements based on expectations, estimates, and projections as of the date of this filing. Actual results and outcomes may differ materially from those expressed in forward-looking statements. See Item 1A of Part I — "Risk Factors." As used herein, "Amazon.com," "we," "our," and similar terms include Amazon.com, Inc. and its subsidiaries, unless the context indicates otherwise.
General
We seek to be Earth's most customer-centric company. We are guided by four principles: customer obsession rather than competitor focus, passion for invention, commitment to operational excellence, and long-term thinking. In each of our segments, we serve our primary customer sets, consisting of consumers, sellers, developers, enterprises, content creators, advertisers, and employees.
We have organized our operations into three segments: North America, International, and Amazon Web Services ("AWS"). These segments reflect the way the Company evaluates its business performance and manages its operations. Information on our net sales is contained in Item 8 of Part II, "Financial Statements and Supplementary Data — Note 10 — Segment Information."
Consumers
We serve consumers through our online and physical stores and focus on selection, price, and convenience. We design our stores to enable hundreds of millions of unique products to be sold by us and by third parties across dozens of product categories. Customers access our offerings through our websites, mobile apps, Alexa, devices, streaming, and physically visiting our stores. We also manufacture and sell electronic devices, including Kindle, Fire tablet, Fire TV, Echo, Ring, Blink, and eero, and we develop and produce media content. We seek to offer our customers low prices, fast and free delivery, easy-to-use functionality, and timely customer service. In addition, we offer subscription services such as Amazon P
…[truncated 3148 chars]…
in digital or other formats. We believe that the principal competitive factors in our retail businesses include selection, price, and convenience, including fast and reliable fulfillment. Additional competitive factors for our seller and enterprise services include the quality, speed, and reliability of our services and tools, as well as customers' ability and willingness to change business practices. Some of our current and potential competitors have greater resources, longer histories, more customers, greater brand recognition, and greater control over inputs critical to our various businesses. They may secure better terms from suppliers, adopt more aggressive pricing, pursue restrictive distribution agreements that restrict our access to supply, direct consumers to their own offerings instead of ours, lock-in potential customers with restrictive terms, and devote more resources to technology, infrastructure, fulfillment, and marketing. The Internet facilitates competitive entry and comparison shopping, which enhances the ability of new, smaller, or lesser-known businesses to compete against us. Each of our businesses is also subject to rapid change and the development of new business models and the entry of new and well-funded competitors. Other companies also may enter into business combinations or alliances that strengthen their competitive positions.
Intellectual Property
We regard our trademarks, service marks, copyrights, patents, domain names, trade dress, trade secrets, proprietary technologies, and similar intellectual property as critical to our success, and we rely on trademark, copyright, and patent law, trade-secret protection, and confidentiality and/or license agreements with our employees, customers, partners, and others to protect our proprietary rights. We have registered, or applied for the registration of, a number of U.S. and international domain names, trademarks, service marks, and copyrights. Additionally, we have filed U.S. and international patent applications covering certain of our proprietary technology.
Seasonality
Our business is affected by seasonality, which historically has resulted in higher sales volume during our fourth quarter, which ends December 31.
Human Capital
Our employees are critical to our mission of being Earth's most customer-centric company. As of December 31, 2022, we employed approximately 1,541,000 full-time and part-time employees. Additionally, we use independent contractors and temporary personnel to supplement our workforce. Competition for qualified personnel is intense, particularly for software engineers, computer scientists, and other technical staff, and constrained labor markets have increased competition for personnel across other parts of our business.
As we strive to be Earth's best employer, we focus on investment and innovation, inclusion and diversity, safety, and engagement to hire and develop the best talent. We rely on numerous and evolving initiatives to implement these objectives and invent mechanisms for talent development, including competitive pay and benefits, flexible work arrangements, and skills training and educational programs such as Amazon Career Choice (funded education for hourly employees) and the Amazon Technical Academy (software development engineer training). Over 100,000 Amazon employees around the world have participated in Career Choice. We also continue to inspect and refine the mechanisms we use to hire, develop, evaluate, and retain our employees to promote equity for all candidates and employees. In addition, safety is integral to everything we do at Amazon and we continue to invest in safety improvements such as capital improvements, new safety technology, vehicle safety controls, and engineering ergonomic solutions. Our safety team is dedicated to using the science of safety to solve complex problems and establish new industry best practices. We also provide mentorship and support resources to our employees, and have deployed numerous programs that advance employee engagement, communication, and feedback.

Our investor relations website is amazon.com/ir and we encourage investors to use it as a way of easily finding information about us. We promptly make available on this website, free of charge, the reports that we file or furnish with the Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC"), corporate governance information (including our Code of Business Conduct and Ethics), and select press releases.

AMZN.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: AMZN (Evidence Point-in-Time: 2024-04-01)**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
*   **Revenue & Earnings:** Strong sequential and annual growth. Q4 2023 revenue was $169.96B, +18.8% QoQ and +13.9% YoY. TTM revenue was $574.78B. Net income showed a sharp recovery, with Q4 2023 at $10.62B (vs. $278M in Q4 2022), driving TTM net income to $30.43B. Quarterly EPS diluted improved from negative values in early 2022 to $1.01 in Q4 2023.
*   **Margins:** Profitability improved materially. Q4 2023 gross margin was 45.5% ($77.41B GP / $169.96B rev). The latest reported operating margin (Q4 2023) was 7.8% ($13.21B / $169.96B). Trailing net margin (from valuation ratios) was 5.3%.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Equity grew to $201.88B as of Q4 2023, up from $146.04B in Q4 2022. Total debt was $67.18B. Debt-to-equity was 0.333. *Note: Supplied data shows negative total liabilities for several quarters (e.g., Q2 2023: -$168.60B), which is inconsistent with standard accounting and likely indicates data entry errors in the source; this prevents a reliable quarter-over-quarter balance-sheet trend analysis.*

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** The YoY EPS growth of +4950% is amplified by a very low base in Q4 2022 ($0.02). The trend across the last four quarters (EPS: $0.31, $0.65, $0.94, $1.01) shows consistent sequential improvement, suggesting strengthening core profitability rather than a single non-recurring event. *Free cash flow data is unavailable, preventing direct assessment of cash conversion.*
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Equity has grown consistently, and the debt-to-equity ratio of 0.333 is low. Absolute debt levels have been relatively stable (~$67B). The negative liability figures noted above preclude a precise assessment of leverage trends.

**3. Valuation vs. Company History**
*   Valuation has compressed significantly as earnings have surged. The trailing P/E diluted of 62.2 (as of 2024-03-28) is down from a range of 57.6 to 326.2 over the past year. The P/E stood at 315.18 in July 2023 and 254.59 in May 2023.
*   Price-to-Book (P/B) has expanded from 7.15 in April 2023 to 9.38 currently.
*   Earnings yield is low at 1.6%, reflecting the still-elevated multiple. The net margin (5.3%) and ROE (15.1%) have expanded to their highest levels within the provided valuation history.

**4. Price Level and Return Context**
*   The stock closed at $180.38 on 2024-03-28, near its 1-year high of $181.70 and well above its 1-year low of $97.70.
*   Recent performance is strong: +17.6% over the last quarter (~63 days) and +85.5% over the last year (~252 days).
*   The price trajectory shows momentum, with positive quarterly returns in three of the last four periods. The recent price expansion has occurred alongside the rapid improvement in trailing earnings and margins.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Upside:** Continued revenue growth (TTM $574.78B) coupled with operating leverage could further expand margins. The TTM operating income is $36.85B. A sustained ROE of 15.1% on a growing equity base ($201.88B) supports future earnings power. The stock is above its 1-year price range, indicating positive momentum.
*   **Downside:** Valuation remains high (P/E 62.2, Earnings Yield 1.6%) despite compression. The stock's rapid appreciation (+85.5% YoY) may have priced in near-term earnings recovery. Inconsistent or unavailable data (cash flow, balance sheet liabilities) introduces uncertainty about underlying quality and resilience. The gross margin (47%) and net margin (5.3%) show a large gap, indicating high operating cost or investment levels.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
Fundamentals show a clear positive trajectory: revenue is growing, profitability is surging, and the balance sheet is strong on key debt ratios. However, this improvement is priced in; the stock trades at a high P/E relative to its history and the market, with a low earnings yield. The significant recent price appreciation (+85.5% over one year) has likely captured much of the near-term earnings recovery. The quantitative case presents a high-quality, growing business at a stretched valuation, implying the forward risk/reward is more balanced than the trailing fundamental momentum suggests.
AMZN.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Report: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN)**
**Decision Date: 2024-04-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
Amazon operates as a customer-centric, diversified technology and retail conglomerate organized into three segments: North America, International, and Amazon Web Services (AWS). Its core businesses include e-commerce (online and physical stores), third-party seller services, subscription services (Prime), consumer electronics, and a leading cloud infrastructure platform (AWS). The company competes on selection, price, and convenience, serving consumers, sellers, developers, enterprises, content creators, and advertisers. The franchise is characterized by scale, a vast fulfillment network, and a growing high-margin AWS business. (Source: 10-K, filing_date=2023-02-03)

**2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **Growth & Profitability Outlook:** Amazon is viewed as performing above expectations, with a narrative of becoming "bigger, better, and more profitable." (Source: News, 2024-03-31). The company provided Q1 2024 guidance for net sales growth of 8%-13% and operating income of $8 billion. (Source: Web context, 2024-02-19).
*   **AI & Technology Catalysts:** AWS is highlighted as a key future profitability driver, with generative AI presenting new opportunities. (Source: Web context, 2024-02-22). Amazon is developing new AI chips (Graviton4, Trainium2) to enhance performance. (Source: Web context, 2024-02-25).
*   **Market Position & Sentiment:** Amazon is consistently grouped among the "Magnificent Seven" mega-cap tech stocks, attracting significant investor and media attention. (Source: News, 2024-03-31, 2024-03-30). Institutional investor interest is noted. (Source: News, 2024-03-30).
*   **Regulatory & Legal Challenges:** Multiple lawsuits and regulatory actions are active. Vermont joined a lawsuit alleging Amazon illegally maintains monopoly power, harming sellers and shoppers through high fees. (Source: Web context, 2024-03-15). The FTC has sued Amazon over subscription practices. (Source: Web context, 2024-03-08). Additional lawsuits allege the company's algorithms direct shoppers to pricier items. (Source: Web context, 2024-02-12, 2024-02-13).

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Regulatory & Legal Overhang:** Significant and ongoing antitrust and consumer protection lawsuits in the U.S. pose a material risk. These could result in operational restrictions, forced business model changes, or substantial financial penalties. (Sources: 2024-03-15, 2024-03-08, 2024-02-12, 2024-02-13).
    *   **Intense Competition:** The company faces broad and intense competition from numerous well-resourced players across all its segments, which could pressure margins and market share. (Source: 10-K, filing_date=2023-02-03).
    *   **Macroeconomic Sensitivity:** Consumer spending and business investment, particularly in cloud services, are subject to economic cycles.
*   **Offsets:**
    *   **AWS & AI Leadership:** The strong growth trajectory and technological leadership of AWS, particularly in generative AI, provide a major offset to retail risks and a path to sustained profitability. (Sources: 2024-02-22, 2024-02-25).
    *   **Scale and Ecosystem:** Amazon's vast customer base, fulfillment infrastructure, and integrated ecosystem (Prime, devices, advertising) create significant competitive moats and cross-selling opportunities. (Source: 10-K, filing_date=2023-02-03).
    *   **Diversified Revenue Streams:** The business model is diversified across retail, cloud, advertising, and subscriptions, reducing reliance on any single segment.

**4. Qualitative Assessment**
**Cautiously Positive.** The core franchise remains powerful, with AWS and AI initiatives representing clear, high-margin growth engines that are actively being developed. Recent narrative and analyst sentiment are constructive on the company's execution and outlook. However, the material and escalating regulatory and legal risks, particularly in the U.S., present a significant and unresolved overhang that could impact long-term strategy and valuation. The balance of evidence points to a strong business facing non-trivial external threats.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Amazon's fundamentals are on a strong positive trajectory with revenue growth of 13.9% YoY, a sharp earnings recovery (Q4 2023 net income $10.62B vs. $278M a year prior), and expanding margins, but the stock's +85.5% one-year appreciation has already priced in much of this improvement, leaving a P/E of 62.2 and earnings yield of just 1.6%. The risk/reward is balanced between durable growth catalysts (AWS, AI, operating leverage) and meaningful headwinds (elevated valuation, regulatory overhang, limited upside after the run).
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Amazon's fundamentals are on a strong positive trajectory with revenue growth of 13.9% YoY, a sharp earnings recovery (Q4 2023 net income $10.62B vs. $278M a year prior), and expanding margins, but the stock's +85.5% one-year appreciation has already priced in much of this improvement, leaving a P/E of 62.2 and earnings yield of just 1.6%. The risk/reward is balanced between durable growth catalysts (AWS, AI, operating leverage) and meaningful headwinds (elevated valuation, regulatory overhang, limited upside after the run).
key_factors: Revenue growth remains robust: Q4 2023 revenue of $169.96B (+13.9% YoY) with TTM revenue of $574.78B, and Q1 2024 guidance of 8%-13% net sales growth.

Profitability has inflected sharply: TTM net income of $30.43B, Q4 2023 operating margin of 7.8%, and ROE of 15.1% on equity of $201.88B, with EPS improving sequentially for four consecutive quarters.

Valuation is stretched despite compression: trailing P/E of 62.2 and earnings yield of 1.6%, with the stock at $180.38 near its 1-year high after a +85.5% annual gain, leaving limited margin of safety.

Regulatory and legal overhang is material: active FTC lawsuit over subscription practices, Vermont antitrust suit alleging monopoly maintenance, and algorithmic pricing lawsuits create unresolved downside risk to the business model and valuation.

We undertake no obligation to update or revise publicly any forward-looking statements, whether because of new information, future events, or otherwise.
3
Item 1
ITEM 1. BUSINESS
GENERAL
Embracing Our Future
Microsoft is a technology company whose mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. We strive to create local opportunity, growth, and impact in every country around the world. We are creating the platforms and tools, powered by artificial intelligence ("AI"), that deliver better, faster, and more effective solutions to support small and large business competitiveness, improve educational and health outcomes, grow public-sector efficiency,  and empower human ingenuity. From infrastructure and data, to business applications and collaboration, we provide unique, differentiated value to customers.
In a world of increasing economic complexity, AI has the power to revolutionize many types of work. Microsoft is now innovating and expanding our portfolio with AI capabilities to help people and organizations overcome today's challenges and emerge stronger. Customers 
…[truncated 19275 chars]…
ing, and inclusive work environment that enables our global employees to create products and services that further our mission.
8
Item 1
As of June 30, 2023, we employed approximately 221,000 people on a full-time basis, 120,000 in the U.S. and 101,000 internationally. Of the total employed people, 89,000 were in operations, including manufacturing, distribution, product support, and consulting services; 72,000 were in product research and development; 45,000 were in sales and marketing; and 15,000 were in general and administration. Certain employees are subject to collective bargaining agreements.
Our Culture
Microsoft's culture is grounded in growth mindset. This means everyone is on a continuous journey to learn and grow, operating as one company instead of multiple siloed businesses.
Our employee listening systems enable us to gather feedback directly from our workforce to inform our programs and employee needs globally. Employees participate in our Employee Signals surveys, which cover a variety of topics such as thriving, inclusion, team culture, wellbeing, and learning and development. We also collect Daily Signals employee survey responses, giving us real-time insights into ways we can support our employees. In addition to Employee Signals and Daily Signals surveys, we gain insights through onboarding, exit surveys, internal Viva Engage channels, employee Q&A sessions, and our internal AskHR Service support.
Diversity and inclusion are core to our business model, and we hold ourselves accountable for driving global systemic change in our workforce and creating an inclusive work environment. We support multiple highly active Employee Resource Groups for women, families, racial and ethnic minorities, military, people with disabilities, and employees who identify as LGBTQIA+, where employees can go for support, networking, and community-building. As described in our 2022 Proxy Statement, annual performance and compensation reviews of our senior leadership team include an evaluation of their contributions to employee culture and diversity. To ensure accountability over time, we publicly disclose our progress on a multitude of workforce metrics including:
- Detailed breakdowns of gender, racial, and ethnic minority representation in our employee population, with data by job types, levels, and segments of our business.
- Our EEO-1 report (equal employment opportunity).
- Disability representation.
- Pay equity (see details below).
Total Rewards and Pay Equity
We develop dynamic, sustainable, market-driven, and strategic programs with the goal of providing a highly differentiated portfolio to attract, reward, and retain top talent and enable our employees to thrive. These programs reinforce our culture and values such as collaboration and growth mindset. Managers evaluate and recommend rewards based on, for example, how well we leverage the work of others and contribute to the success of our colleagues. We monitor pay equity and career progress across multiple dimensions. Our total compensation opportunity is highly differentiated and is market competitive.
In order to manage our costs in a dynamic, competitive environment, in fiscal year 2023 we announced that base salaries of salaried employees would remain at fiscal year 2022 levels. Pay increases continue to be available for rewards-eligible hourly and equivalent employees. We will continue our practice of investing in stock for all rewards-eligible employees, salaried and hourly, and investing in bonuses for all eligible employees.
9
Item 1
Since 2016, we have reported on pay equity as part of our annual Diversity and Inclusion report. In 2022, we reported that all racial and ethnic minority employees in the U.S. combined earn $1.008 for every $1.000 earned by their white counterparts, that women in the U.S. earn $1.007 for every $1.000 earned by their counterparts who are men, and that women outside the U.S. earn $1.002 for every $1.000 earned by their counterparts outside the U.S. who are men. In this year's report, we again expanded our pay equity data beyond the U.S. to report on 61 additional countries (up from 12 last year), representing 99.8% of our global Microsoft workforce.
In addition, we began reporting on unadjusted median pay in our annual report, comparing total pay amounts for all employees regardless of factors such as job title, level, or tenure. For employees who are eligible for rewards, the analysis showed that total pay for women is 89.6% of total pay for men in the U.S. and 86.2% outside of the U.S., and total pay for racial and ethnic minorities in the U.S. is 89.9% of total pay for white employees. As we continue to increase representation for women and racial and ethnic minorities at more senior levels, and continue to ensure pay equity for all, the gap between the medians will reduce.
Our intended result is a global performance and development approach that fosters our culture, and competitive compensation that ensures equitable pay by role while supporting pay for performance.
Wellbeing and Hybrid Work
Microsoft is committed to supporting our employees' wellbeing while they are at work and in their personal lives. We have invested significantly in wellbeing, and offer a differentiated benefits package which includes many physical, emotional, and financial wellness programs including counseling through the Microsoft CARES Employee Assistance Program, mental wellbeing support, flexible fitness benefits, disability accommodations, savings and investment tools, adoption assistance, and back-up care for children and elders. Finally, our Occupational Health and Safety program helps ensure employees can stay safe while they are working.
We introduced Hybrid Workplace Flexibility Guidance to better support leaders, managers, and employees in hybrid work scenarios. Our ongoing survey data shows that 93% of employees value the flexibility related to work location, work site, and work hours, and 78% are satisfied with the quality of connection with co-workers. There is no one-size-fits-all approach to flexible work at Microsoft. As a company, we will continue to leverage data and research to inform decision making, balancing the needs of business, team, and individual.
Learning and Development
We offer a range of learning opportunities, including personalized opportunities on our internal and external learning portals, in-classroom learning, required learning on compliance and company culture, on-the-job advancement opportunities, and manager coaching. We also provide customized manager learning, new employee orientation, and tools for operating in a flexible hybrid work environment.
All Microsoft employees globally access our single Viva Learning tool for both required and personal choice learning. This includes courses focused on our core principles and compliance matters, such as Business Conduct, Privacy, Security Foundations, and Harassment Prevention. We also deliver skills training for employees based on their profession and role discipline.
We have over 27,000 people managers, all of whom must complete between 20-33 hours of compulsory training on leadership and management and are assigned additional targeted training on an ongoing basis related to people management, compliance, and culture.

OPERATING SEGMENTS
We operate our business and report our financial performance using three segments: Productivity and Business Processes, Intelligent Cloud, and More Personal Computing. Our segments provide management with a comprehensive financial view of our key businesses. The segments enable the alignment of strategies and objectives across the development, sales, marketing, and services organizations, and they provide a framework for timely and rational allocation of resources within businesses.

MSFT.quantitative_report:
**MSFT Quantitative Report**  
*Decision date: 2024-04-01 (evidence point-in-time ≤ 2024-03-28)*  

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**  
- **Revenue**: Latest quarter (Q2 2023-12-31) revenue = $62.02B, up +17.6% YoY from $52.75B. QoQ growth = +9.7% from $56.52B. TTM revenue = $227.58B.  
- **Earnings**: Latest EPS diluted = $2.93, up +33.2% YoY from $2.20. QoQ change = -2.0% from $2.99. TTM net income = $82.54B.  
- **Margins**: Latest gross margin = 68.4% (gross profit $42.40B / revenue $62.02B). Operating margin = 43.6% (operating income $27.03B / revenue $62.02B). TTM operating income = $100.53B, implying TTM operating margin ≈ 44.2%.  
- **Cash-Flow**: No quarterly cash-flow data supplied.  
- **Balance Sheet**: Total assets grew from $364.55B (2022-12-31) to $470.56B (2023-12-31). Total equity increased from $183.14B to $238.27B. Total debt slightly decreased from $48.12B to $47.18B. Cash declined from $99.51B to $81.02B.  

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**  
- **Earnings Quality**: YoY EPS growth (+33.2%) exceeds revenue growth (+17.6%), indicating operational leverage. However, QoQ EPS declined -2.0% despite +9.7% revenue growth, suggesting cost volatility (Q2 cost of revenue $19.62B vs. Q1 $16.30B). Net margin = 36.3% (TTM).  
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Debt-to-equity = 0.198. Cash ($81.02B) covers total debt ($47.18B) by 1.72x. Total equity ($238.27B) exceeds total liabilities ($232.29B).  

**3. Valuation Versus Own History**  
- Latest valuation ratios (2024-03-28): PE diluted = 38.065 (range 29.8–39.7), EV/EBIT = 30.916 (range 23.6–31.6), P/B = 13.187 (range 10.6–13.9), net margin = 0.363, ROE = 0.346.  
- Historical progression (oldest to newest): PE diluted expanded from 31.824 (2023-04-03) to 38.065; EV/EBIT from 25.298 to 30.916; P/B from 11.721 to 13.187.  
- Current PE and EV/EBIT are near the top of their 365-day ranges.  

**4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context**  
- Price (2024-03-28) = $420.72. 52-week high = $430.82, low = $275.37.  
- Quarterly return (63 trading days) = +12.5%. Annual return (252 trading days) = +52.9%.  
- Quarterly path: +22.0% (Q2 2023), -6.9% (Q3), +19.6% (Q4), +12.5% (Q1 2024).  
- Price appreciation (+52.9% annual) significantly outpaces YoY revenue growth (+17.6%) and EPS growth (+33.2%), reflecting multiple expansion.  

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**  
- **Upside**: Strong YoY earnings growth (+33.2%) and high margins (gross margin 68.4%, operating margin 43.6%) could sustain if revenue momentum persists. Balance sheet resilience (debt-to-equity 0.198, cash > debt) provides stability.  
- **Downside**: Valuation is stretched (PE 38.065 at range top); QoQ EPS decline (-2.0%) signals potential cost pressures. Price momentum (+52.9% annual) may not be sustainable if growth decelerates, risking multiple contraction.  

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**  
MSFT exhibits strong fundamental momentum: double-digit revenue growth, expanding earnings, and high margins with a resilient balance sheet. However, valuation metrics are near historical highs, and recent price appreciation has outpaced underlying growth (PE expanded from ~31.8 to 38.1). The risk/reward appears balanced: continued growth could justify elevated multiples, but any slowdown may trigger valuation contraction given the stretched current levels. The absence of cash-flow data limits full assessment of cash generation quality.
MSFT.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: MSFT**
**Decision Date: 2024-04-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Description (from 2023-07-27 10-K)**
Microsoft's mission is to empower every person and organization. Its strategy is built on three interconnected ambitions: reinventing productivity and business processes (Microsoft 365, Dynamics 365, LinkedIn), building the intelligent cloud and intelligent edge platform (Azure, Nuance, mixed reality), and creating more personal computing (Windows, Search, Devices, Gaming). A core strategic pillar is the integration of AI ("copilot") across its entire portfolio. The company identifies its long-term partnership with OpenAI, for which it is the exclusive cloud provider, as central to its AI innovation and competitive advantage. The business is structured into three segments: Productivity and Business Processes, Intelligent Cloud, and More Personal Computing.

**2. Recent Developments (Supplied Text, Dated)**
*   **AI Strategy & Positioning (2024-03-24):** News coverage states Microsoft has "benefited from its early focus on the shift to artificial intelligence (AI)" and was "quick to adopt and integrate generative AI tools."
*   **AI Product Adoption (2024-03-27):** Reports indicate major customers like Coca-Cola are adopting Microsoft's AI copilots and Azure services.
*   **AI Partnership Scrutiny (2024-02-27):** Microsoft's deal with Mistral AI is facing EU regulatory scrutiny, according to Reuters.
*   **AI Safety & Ethics Concerns (2024-03-06):** A Microsoft employee raised concerns to the U.S. FTC about the potential for Microsoft's AI tools to create harmful content, including sexualized images.
*   **Regulatory Action (2024-03-11):** The European Data Protection Supervisor found that the European Commission's use of Microsoft 365 infringed data protection rules.
*   **Competitive Dynamics (2024-03-30):** Tech giants including Intel, Qualcomm, Arm, and Google are forming a new AI alliance, posing a potential competitive threat.
*   **Valuation Commentary (2024-03-31):** Multiple news sources note Microsoft's stock is at its most expensive on a trailing price-to-earnings basis in nearly two decades.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Regulatory and Antitrust Headwinds.** Evidence points to ongoing regulatory scrutiny in the EU for AI partnerships (Mistral AI, 2024-02-27) and data protection (2024-03-11). A retrospective article (2024-03-25) also frames this as a persistent risk, referencing Microsoft's historic antitrust litigation. **Offset:** The 10-K describes a broad, integrated compliance and security framework to navigate evolving regulations.
*   **Risk: AI Execution, Safety, and Ethical Challenges.** Internal concerns about AI safety and harmful outputs have been raised (2024-03-06). **Offset:** The 10-K outlines Microsoft's commitment to "responsible AI" guided by principles of fairness, reliability, and safety.
*   **Risk: Competitive Threat in AI.** A competitor alliance is forming (2024-03-30), and analysts suggest startups like Palantir could disrupt Microsoft's AI position (2024-03-31). **Offset:** Microsoft's scale, its entrenched enterprise relationships, and the early-mover integration of AI (noted 2024-03-24) are cited as defensive strengths.
*   **Risk: Valuation Sensitivity.** Multiple reports highlight elevated valuation multiples (2024-03-31), making the stock potentially sensitive to any growth disappointments.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral.** The narrative confirms Microsoft's strong franchise with durable competitive advantages in cloud and productivity, significantly bolstered by a leading early position in generative AI integration. Recent developments show continued adoption by major enterprises (positive). However, the near-term risk profile is heightened by tangible regulatory scrutiny in key markets, emerging competitive dynamics, and explicit concerns about elevated valuation. These risks partially offset the positive growth narrative, leading to a balanced qualitative view.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Microsoft's fundamental momentum remains strong with 17.6% YoY revenue growth and 33.2% EPS growth, but the stock's 52.9% annual price appreciation has pushed valuation to near two-decade highs (PE 38.1), leaving limited margin of safety. The AI-driven growth narrative is compelling but partially priced in, and regulatory headwinds in the EU add incremental risk.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Microsoft's fundamental momentum remains strong with 17.6% YoY revenue growth and 33.2% EPS growth, but the stock's 52.9% annual price appreciation has pushed valuation to near two-decade highs (PE 38.1), leaving limited margin of safety. The AI-driven growth narrative is compelling but partially priced in, and regulatory headwinds in the EU add incremental risk.
key_factors: Strong fundamental growth: Q2 revenue of $62.02B grew 17.6% YoY and EPS of $2.93 grew 33.2% YoY, with operating margins of 43.6% and a resilient balance sheet (debt-to-equity 0.198, cash of $81B covering debt 1.72x).

Stretched valuation: PE diluted of 38.065 is near the top of its 365-day range (29.8-39.7), and price appreciation of 52.9% over the past year significantly outpaces revenue and earnings growth, reflecting multiple expansion rather than pure fundamental improvement.

AI positioning as a double-edged sword: Microsoft's early AI integration and enterprise adoption (e.g., Coca-Cola copilot adoption, 2024-03-27) is a competitive advantage, but EU regulatory scrutiny of the Mistral AI deal (2024-02-27) and data protection findings (2024-03-11) create near-term headwinds.

QoQ earnings softness: Despite 9.7% QoQ revenue growth, EPS declined 2.0% QoQ from $2.99 to $2.93, with cost of revenue rising from $16.30B to $19.62B, suggesting potential margin pressure that warrants monitoring.
